task: I need to send a short follow-up to a customer after a delayed update, but I don’t want it to sound stiff or overdone. Can you draft something I could use, and keep it to a couple of sentences?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple version:

“Just following up on the delay update I sent earlier. I’m still checking on the latest timing and will let you know as soon as I have something confirmed.”

If you want, I can make it a little warmer or a little firmer depending on the customer.

user_simulator.text: Pretty close, but that still sounds a little canned. Give me 2-3 options that feel more natural, like something I’d actually send to a customer without sounding scripted.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few more natural options:

1. **“Just wanted to follow up on the delay update I sent earlier. I’m still checking on the latest timing and will send you an update as soon as I have something confirmed.”**

2. **“Quick follow-up on the delay — I’m still looking into the latest timing and will let you know as soon as I have a clear update.”**

3. **“Wanted to circle back on the delay update. I’m still checking on it and will reach out as soon as I have more solid timing.”**

If you want, I can also make them a little more **firm**, **more apologetic**, or **more customer-service casual**.

user_simulator.text:
Closer, yeah. Still a little too polished though — “just wanted to follow up” and “circle back” both sound like office-speak.

Give me 2-3 versions that are a little more plainspoken, like I’d actually type them to a customer. Short, direct, respectful.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few plainer versions:

1. **“I wanted to check back on the delay update. I’m still looking into the timing and will let you know as soon as I have something solid.”**

2. **“Quick update on the delay — I’m still checking on the timing and will send you a note as soon as I know more.”**

3. **“Still working on the delay update. I’ll let you know as soon as I have a confirmed timing.”**

If you want, I can make them even more plain or a little warmer.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, better. Still a hair too tidy though — #2 is probably the closest, but I’d want them even more like plain text, not a template. Give me 2-3 that sound like a real person typing fast, respectful, and not trying too hard.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here are a few more plain, quick versions:

1. **“Quick update on the delay — I’m still checking on timing and will let you know as soon as I have it.”**

2. **“Still working through the delay update. I’ll send something over as soon as I have a confirmed timing.”**

3. **“Just a quick note — I’m still looking into the timing and will follow up as soon as I know more.”**

If you want, I can make them a little more direct or a little warmer.

user_simulator.text: #1 is the closest. Still feels a little cleaned up though — I’d want it more like plain text, less like it got drafted. Give me 2-3 that sound more like a real quick message someone would type on their phone, still polite, not stiff.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here are a few that are a little more like a quick phone message:

1. **“Quick update on the delay — still checking on timing and I’ll let you know as soon as I have it.”**

2. **“Still looking into the delay update. I’ll send something over once I have confirmed timing.”**

3. **“Just checking back on the delay — I’m still on it and will follow up as soon as I know more.”**

If you want, I can make them even more casual or a little more customer-service polished.

user_simulator.text: #1 is basically there, but I’d trim it one more step — less “quick update,” more just plain text. Something like: “Still checking on the delay timing. I’ll let you know as soon as I have it.” Give me 2-3 in that lane.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are a few in that lane:

1. **“Still checking on the delay timing. I’ll let you know as soon as I have it.”**

2. **“Still looking into the delay timing. I’ll send an update as soon as I have something.”**

3. **“Checking on the timing now. I’ll follow up as soon as I know more.”**

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s basically it. #1 works. #2 is fine too. I’d probably use one of those as-is.
